free_text: Yield Measurement Using Godiva IV Critical Assembly
Godiva IV is a critical assembly
composed of unreflected highly enriched uranium (HEU)
Prompt bursts of fast fission neutrons of around 24
microsec FWHM in duration with a peak neutron flux of
The neptunium samples were made from a solution of
NpO2(NO3) and dried in quartz ampoules. From the
gamma spectra, the masses were determined to be 5.8
mg, 10.7 mg, 20.0 mg, and 42.89 mg for Np1, Np2, Np3,
The setup consisted of two Broad-Energy
each coupled to a Lynx data acquisition system.
Fission was induced in a NpO2(NO3) target
using the Godiva IV critical assembly in burst mode.
The irradiated sample was transferred to a
minutes. gamma-ray list mode data was collected from
50 minutes to 1 week after the irradiation. gamma-ray
spectroscopy was performed to analyze the time
dependent gamma-ray yields using an automated peak
search algorithm to identify isotopes by their decay
gamma-ray energy and half-life. The initial activity
for each isotope identified was used to calculate
Cumulative fission product yield results from two
detectors. Values were combined by an uncertainty
Total errors were calculated from error
propagations.  This work uncertainty budget is
affected by the quality of nuclear data, in
particular, very large errors in branching ratios.
All other errors are rather small, less than 1.5%.
Witness foil reactions were corrected to the burst
irradiation time. Data were corrected for target
